title: testnet_scan_advances_tip has no timeout
description:
wallet/tests/e2e_testnet.rs, testnet_scan_advances_tip (Test 3): the scanner.scan_to_tip().await call is not wrapped in tokio::time::timeout. If the lightwalletd server becomes unresponsive during the 5-block scan, the test hangs indefinitely with no way to recover short of killing the process.

Contrast with Test 4 (testnet_shielded_payment_end_to_end), which correctly wraps Bob's scan_to_tip() in tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(BOB_SCAN_SECS), ...).

Test 3:
    let blocks_scanned = scanner.scan_to_tip().await.expect("scan_to_tip must succeed");  // no timeout

Fix: wrap the call exactly as Test 4 does:
    let blocks_scanned = tokio::time::timeout(
        Duration::from_secs(SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECS),
        scanner.scan_to_tip(),
    )
    .await
    .expect("scan must complete within timeout")
    .expect("scan_to_tip must succeed");

Add a SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECS constant alongside the other timeout constants at the top of the file.
